paper_title: The Effects of Temperature on the Turnover of δ18O and δD in Juvenile Corn Snakes (Elaphe guttata): A Novel Study with Ecological Implications

methods.description: This method describes the setup for housing corn snakes under different temperature treatments to study the effects of temperature on δ18O and δD turnover.

steps.description: Corn snakes were divided into three temperature treatments: 24°C, 27°C, and 30°C, with a control group allowed to freely thermoregulate.
steps.searchable_summary: divide snakes into temperature treatment groups

steps.description: Snakes were housed in separate containers with random location changes within environmental chambers to eliminate temperature or light gradient effects.
steps.searchable_summary: house snakes in separate containers with random location changes

steps.description: Lighting was controlled with a 12-hour light/dark timer, with different placements for temperature cabinets and thermoregulating group.
Snakes were provided with local Provo, Utah tap water and fed one pinkie per week.
steps.searchable_summary: provide local tap water and feed snakes weekly

methods.description: This method outlines the collection and preparation of snake scale samples for isotope analysis.

steps.description: Scales were collected from thawed specimens by cutting a one-inch section of the tail.
steps.searchable_summary: collect scales from thawed snake specimens

steps.description: Scales were rinsed with petroleum ether and distilled water, then dried and stored.

methods.description: This method describes the analysis of δD and δ18O in snake scales using isotope ratio mass spectrometry.

steps.description: A 1-mg sample from each tissue or substrate was analyzed for δD and δ18O using isotope ratio mass spectrometry.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze δD and δ18O using IRMS

methods.description: This method involves statistical tests to analyze the effects of temperature and time on δD and δ18O changes.

steps.description: Effects of temperature and time on δD and δ18O were tested using multiple linear regression.
Pearson's correlation tests were calculated for temperature, weight at death, and δD and δ18O.
Data were normalized and analyzed using ANCOVA to determine significant differences.
